name: Formula One 1985
attribution: Historical data derived from f1db (github.com/f1db/f1db, CC BY 4.0)
AI ratings from the community F-Retro_Gen3.xml custom-AI set (jusk), matching Klukkluk's F1 1985 skinpack
notes: Coverage: f1db lists Manfred Winkelhock (ram, rounds 1-9) but he died before round 10; his RAM #9 stint is included through round 9 and Philippe Alliot inherits the #9 car (rounds 10-14) as the source AI set does.
Coverage: Stefan Bellof (tyrrell, rounds 2-11) died before the Belgian GP; his Tyrrell #4 stint is included through round 11, matching f1db and the source AI set.

Placeholder venues (isPlaceholder=true): Detroit -> Long Beach, Circuit Paul Ricard -> Le Mans Bugatti, 1984 Nurburgring GP-Strecke -> modern Nurburgring GP (Veedol), and Zandvoort -> Zolder (not in AMS2). Lap counts are recomputed to preserve the real race distance.

The source custom-AI XML carries per-track (tracks="...") substitution overrides for reserve drivers sharing a car number; those stints are modelled here as separate round-scoped entries rather than carried into aiOverrides.
